features.summary: Occidental reported record Q1 2022 earnings with $2.12 adjusted EPS and $3.3 billion in free cash flow, more than double Q1 2021 levels. The company repaid $3.3 billion in debt during the quarter and an additional $300 million post-quarter, progressing toward their $5 billion debt reduction target. Production averaged 1.1 million BOE/day in line with guidance, with international operations completing scheduled turnarounds. OxyChem delivered record quarterly earnings driven by strong caustic, chlorine and PVC pricing, while midstream outperformed on higher gas processing margins and optimization opportunities.
features.management_outlook: Management expects to complete the $5 billion debt reduction target and initiate the $3 billion share buyback program in Q2 2022. Production is expected to grow from Q2 through year-end, maintaining 2022 average production in line with 2021 levels. OxyChem is projected to achieve a fourth consecutive record quarter in Q2, though market conditions may soften in the second half. The company raised full-year guidance for OxyChem and midstream earnings, while maintaining overall production guidance despite reducing Colorado drilling activity due to permitting delays.
features.risks: Supply chain constraints and inflation pressures continue to impact costs, with OCTG prices up over 100% and sand costs rising significantly. Colorado permitting delays forced reallocation of drilling activity from Rockies to Permian, though 2022 production targets remain unchanged. Rising interest rates could impact housing demand and affect OxyChem's PVC business in the second half. International production faces headwinds from higher oil prices under production sharing contracts, though this is expected to be offset by outperformance in other segments.
features.key_drivers: Strong commodity pricing environment with WTI expectations around $90/barrel versus original $73 Brent budget drove significant cash flow generation. OxyChem benefited from tight supply/demand fundamentals in caustic and PVC markets, with domestic PVC demand up 10% year-over-year. Operational excellence delivered strong well performance in Permian and Rockies, with 23-well pads and 15,000+ foot laterals exceeding expectations. Debt reduction progress positions the company for potential investment grade rating upgrade, with all three major agencies rating debt one notch below investment grade.
